channel.title: elizaOS Daily Intelligence
channel.description: Daily facts and insights from the elizaOS ecosystem

item.title: elizaOS Intelligence: 2026-02-17
item.description:
Summary: The ecosystem is currently navigating a period of rapid technical decentralization with the launch of the MoltBridge trust layer and Security Oracle API, contrasted by significant community friction regarding the finalized ai16z token migration.

GitHub Activity:

  • feat: next generation multi-language Eliza with Rust, Python and TypeScript support (open)
  • feat(scenarios): Add Cost Evaluator (closed)
  • feat(auth): implement JWT authentication and user management (merged)
  • [Bug] URL in message triggers duplicate LLM calls (open)

Community Highlights:

  • #coders: Focus on the launch of MoltBridge and the Security Oracle API. Significant interest in A2A trust layers and Kalshi prediction market integration....
  • #discussion: Heavy debate over the closed token migration window and the ethics/rules of autonomous AI agents participating in Discord....

item.title: elizaOS Intelligence: 2026-02-16
item.description:
Summary: The ElizaOS project is navigating a community crisis regarding the closure of the ai16z token migration window while simultaneously shifting toward a multi-language architecture (Rust, Python, TypeScript). Technical progress includes the completion of the Openclaw adapter and the introduction of a global JWT authentication system for enhanced security.

GitHub Activity:

  • feat: next generation multi-language Eliza with Rust, Python and TypeScript support (open)
  • feat(auth): implement JWT authentication and user management (merged)
  • [Bug] URL in message triggers duplicate LLM calls (open)

Community Highlights:

  • #discussion: Dominated by frustrations regarding the missed ai16z-to-elizaOS migration deadline. Users are requesting manual overrides for long-term holders while moderators maintain that the window is closed....
  • #coders: Technical discussion on ecosystem growth strategy, specifically suggesting that base installs should include hints making agents aware of ElizaCloud capabilities....

item.title: elizaOS Intelligence: 2026-02-15
item.description:
Summary: The 90-day ai16z to elizaOS token migration window has officially closed, transitioning the community focus toward v2.0 multi-language framework support and global authentication hardening. Technical efforts are currently centered on cloud credit administration and resolving runtime errors in the bootstrap plugin.

GitHub Activity:

  • feat: next generation multi-language Eliza with Rust, Python and TypeScript support (open)
  • feat(auth): implement JWT authentication and user management (merged)
  • fix: Add null/undefined checks to prevent Object.entries errors in plugin-bootstrap (open)
  • [Bug] URL in message triggers duplicate LLM calls (open)

Community Highlights:

  • #core-devs: Focus on cloud administration and database-level credit management. Resolved issues finding OAuth-based accounts using organization slugs....
  • #discussion: Clarification of the token migration deadline. Support tickets are only being processed for those who applied before the 90-day cutoff....
  • #arch-and-internal: Debate over wallet management architectures, with a preference for HD wallets over heavy Privy-based OAuth approaches....

item.title: elizaOS Intelligence: 2026-02-14
item.description:
Summary: ElizaOS is transitioning to a multi-language architecture supporting Rust and Python while implementing significant security hardening via JWT authentication. Community attention is divided between these technical advancements and critical UX gaps regarding non-technical user onboarding and token utility clarity.

GitHub Activity:

  • feat: next generation multi-language Eliza with Rust, Python and TypeScript support (open)
  • feat(auth): implement JWT authentication and user management (merged)
  • feat: ActionFilterService — vector search + BM25 reranking (merged)
  • [Bug] URL in message triggers duplicate LLM calls (open)

Community Highlights:

  • #core-devs: Demonstration of tip.md and TipCat PR rating system at Clawcon HK. Discussion on rewarding developers for code documentation....
  • #discussion: Technical deep dive into Moltbook anti-bot verification solutions and debate over ElizaOS's accessibility for non-technical users....
  • #coders: Discussion on 3D model format conversion (.glb to .vrm) and troubleshooting agent posting errors on X related to roomId....

item.title: elizaOS Intelligence: 2026-02-13
item.description:
Summary: ElizaOS is transitioning to a multi-language architecture (Rust, Python, TypeScript) and hardening security with a new JWT authentication system. Concurrently, core developers are consolidating the plugin ecosystem and addressing UX friction in the ElizaCloud platform.

GitHub Activity:

  • feat: next generation multi-language Eliza with Rust, Python and TypeScript support (open)
  • feat(core): add request context for per-user entity settings (merged)
  • [Bug] URL in message triggers duplicate LLM calls (open)

Community Highlights:

  • #core-devs: Technical focus on repository consolidation for n8n plugins and managing the transition from v1 to v2 plugins while maintaining quality parity....
  • #coders: Evaluation of agent wallet architectures, contrasting Privy-style OAuth apps against Hierarchical Deterministic (HD) wallets for better flexibility....

item.title: elizaOS Intelligence: 2026-02-12
item.description:
Summary: Development focus shifted toward agent authentication (SIWE) and multi-language support (Rust/Python) while the community remains fixated on UX barriers for non-coders and manual token migration delays.

GitHub Activity:

  • feat: next generation multi-language Eliza with Rust, Python and TypeScript support (open)
  • feat(auth): implement JWT authentication and user management (merged)
  • [Bug] URL in message triggers duplicate LLM calls (open)

Community Highlights:

  • #dc-coders: Technical focus on ElizaCloud development, specifically agent-based authentication using SIWE and exploring API-key generation for autonomous agents....
  • #dc-discussion: Heavy user feedback on ElizaCloud platform friction and ongoing anxiety regarding the manual token migration process for late users....

item.title: elizaOS Intelligence: 2026-02-11
item.description:
Summary: The project is navigating a transition to v2.0.0 with multi-language support (Rust/Python) while addressing significant community tensions regarding token utility and milestone-based communication transparency.

GitHub Activity:

  • ActionFilterService — vector search + BM25 reranking (open)
  • fix: Add null/undefined checks to prevent Object.entries errors (open)

Community Highlights:

  • #discussion: Tension over the lack of a scheduled roadmap and regular updates vs. the current milestone-based communication approach....
  • #coders: Technical discussions regarding OpenClaw capabilities, system command execution, and recursive recruitment for agentic payment apps....

item.title: elizaOS Intelligence: 2026-02-10
item.description:
Summary: Discussions shifted towards the launch of the 'Milaidy' personal assistant and major database optimizations, while community tension remained high due to token performance and Korean exchange delisting rumors.

GitHub Activity:

  • ActionFilterService — vector search + BM25 reranking for action/provider filtering (open)
  • feat(core): add request context for per-user entity settings (merged)
  • [Bug] URL in message triggers duplicate LLM calls (open)

Community Highlights:

  • #discussion: Heavy focus on clearing up misinformation regarding Korean exchange delistings and defending the team against claims of developer exits and token sales....
  • #coders: Technical focus on the Milaidy project launch, VPS installation bugs, and proposed directory structures utilizing PM2 as an external process manager....

item.title: elizaOS Intelligence: 2026-02-09
item.description:
Summary: Technical focus is split between optimizing the core Eliza framework's database performance and the active development of 'milaidy', a Mac-native application. Simultaneously, the project faces severe community tension regarding token delistings in Korea and significant price declines.

GitHub Activity:

  • feat: next generation multi-language Eliza with Rust, Python and TypeScript support (open)
  • feat(plugin-bootstrap): comprehensive optimization and robustness improvements (merged)
  • feat: ActionFilterService — vector search + BM25 reranking (open)

Community Highlights:

  • #coders: Discussions focused on milaidy integration, specifically plugin compatibility and character configuration bugs. Architecture proposals for using PM2 for process management were introduced....
  • #core-devs: Critical identification of PostgreSQL performance degradation caused by excessive hits to the logs table. Stan confirmed an optimization plan is underway....
  • #discussion: High community frustration regarding the token price drop to $0.0013 and delistings on Korean exchanges. Concerns were raised about project transparency and missing deliverables....

item.title: elizaOS Intelligence: 2026-02-08
item.description:
Summary: The community focus shifted toward the launch of Milaidy, a Mac-native AI assistant, while critical login and credit bugs on ElizaCloud were addressed. Strategically, the project is moving from text-heavy prompts toward logic-based synchronization to improve agent efficiency.

GitHub Activity:

  • dynamic execution engine (test if context is going to blown) (merged)
  • feat(core): add request context for per-user entity settings (merged)
  • [Agent] Eliza Character File & Prompt Engineering (open)

Community Highlights:

  • #core-devs: Focus was on the Milaidy project's architecture and the strategic debate over branding Eliza-derivative projects vs. maintaining core brand mindshare....
  • #coders: Heavy recruitment of community developers for bug hunting in the Milaidy repository and testing 'next-gen' Eliza software....

item.title: elizaOS Intelligence: 2026-02-07
item.description:
Summary: ElizaOS reached a major milestone with its expansion to Ethereum as a cross-chain platform, while simultaneously launching the production-ready Babylon game for internal testing. However, the ecosystem faces immediate technical challenges with ElizaCloud account fragmentation and a contentious closure of the 90-day token migration window.

GitHub Activity:

  • V2.0.0: dynamic execution engine (merged)
  • feat(core): add request context for per-user entity settings (merged)
  • [Infra] Telegram Webhook Registration (closed)

Community Highlights:

  • #coders: Technical focus on ElizaCloud payment and account bugs. Users are being redirected to dev environments, causing balance and credit issues....
  • #core-devs: Launch and immediate hot-fixing of the Babylon game. Testing centered on profile updates and admin access through ElizaLabs credentials....
  • #discussion: Heated debate over the closed token migration window and announcements regarding Ethereum cross-chain expansion....

item.title: elizaOS Intelligence: 2026-02-06
item.description:
Summary: The ElizaOS ecosystem is facing critical technical challenges on the ElizaCloud platform alongside community friction regarding the closed 90-day token migration window. Simultaneous development pushes include the internal launch of Babylon.market and the release of Claude Opus 4.6 integration interests.

GitHub Activity:

  • [Plugin] Integrate SMS/iMessage (closed)
  • [Domain] Purchase eliza.app Domain (closed)
  • feat!: Dynamic MCP tool actions (v1.8.0) (merged)

Community Highlights:

  • #coders: Heavy focus on ElizaCloud bugs, specifically account credit failures and the account-overwriting welcome email bug. Discussions also covered VPN-blocked payment pages....
  • #core-devs: Centrally focused on Babylon.market deployment, fixing broken Discord OAuth and reward systems. Discussion regarding Claude 4.6 release features....
  • #discussion: Community tension peaked regarding the closed token migration window. Scams targeting un-migrated users were detected. Updates on DegenAI and RentASoul were shared....

item.title: elizaOS Intelligence: 2026-02-05
item.description:
Summary: ElizaOS is undergoing a strategic pivot toward revenue-generating products while managing internal team restructuring and technical comparisons with the Openclaw framework. Core development has prioritized OAuth integrations and a version 2.0 framework refresh to improve packaging and developer adoption.

GitHub Activity:

  • [Agent] Eliza Character File & Prompt Engineering (open)
  • [Plugin] Integrate SMS/iMessage (closed)
  • feat!: Dynamic MCP tool actions (v1.8.0) (merged)

Community Highlights:

  • #coders: Technical deep dives into Moltbook's success via heartbeat mechanisms and emotionally manipulative prompts to drive LLM participation. Discussion on ElizaOS 2.0 native skills and autonomous mode defau...
  • #core-devs: Comparison of ElizaOS business focus vs. Openclaw personal assistant focus. Updates on Babylon.market testing bugs including infinite spinners and network switching issues....

item.title: elizaOS Intelligence: 2026-02-04
item.description:
Summary: The community is approaching the critical February 3rd token migration deadline amidst reports of significant financial losses and technical friction in ElizaCloud's payment infrastructure. Technical efforts focus on multi-layered security for plugin execution and the introduction of the large-scale CICADA-71 distributed AI agent challenge.

GitHub Activity:

  • Main eliza repository update (open)
  • [Plugin] Integrate SMS/iMessage (closed)
  • eliza-cloud-v2 update (open)

Community Highlights:

  • #coders: Focus on security for plugin-cskills/OpenClaw. Discussion of scanner skills, LLM review for malicious skills, and sandboxing....
  • #core-devs: Tooling compatibility issues noted for Apple Silicon only apps (Codex). Advice to move development to odi-dev branch....

item.title: elizaOS Intelligence: 2026-02-03
item.description:
Summary: The community is focused on the February 3rd token migration deadline while developers address a 56% failure rate in agent skill invocation using mandatory activation sequences. Strategic shifts prioritize the deployment of Babylon into Trusted Execution Environments (TEE) to capitalize on a projected 3-month hype cycle.

GitHub Activity:

  • feat!: Dynamic MCP tool actions (v1.8.0) (merged)
  • [Plugin] Integrate SMS/iMessage (closed)
  • [Domain] Purchase eliza.app Domain (closed)

Community Highlights:

  • #core-devs: Technical debate on skill invocation reliability; consensus moved towards mandatory reasoning steps and better documentation via AGENTS.md....
  • #coders: Discussions on making ElizaCloud more accessible to non-technical users and addressing visual consistency bugs in image generation....

item.title: elizaOS Intelligence: 2026-02-02
item.description:
Summary: The ElizaOS ecosystem is navigating a critical transition period marked by the February 3rd token migration deadline and strategic pivot toward the 'Babylon' social agent platform. While technical infrastructure is expanding through GPU training workflows and N8N integrations, community concerns persist regarding ElizaCloud payment friction and team wallet transparency.

GitHub Activity:

  • feat!: Dynamic MCP tool actions (v1.8.0) (merged)
  • Monday Launch - Eliza App MVP (open)
  • Corrected installation instructions to use @elizaos/cli (merged)

Community Highlights:

  • #discussion: Focus on the impending February 3rd migration deadline. Users are reporting technical friction with wallet balances, while the team clarifies the VC SAFT status and a dynamic 6-8 month runway....
  • #coders: Discussions on ElizaCloud API friction, specifically the requirement for credit cards on free tiers. Technical debates compared the multi-agent architecture of Eliza to the personal assistant focus of...
  • #core-devs: Coordination of GPU training deployment using Docker Compose and exploring PageIndex for tree-based RAG systems. Integration of Trendshift badges into the repository README was discussed....

item.title: elizaOS Intelligence: 2026-02-01
item.description:
Summary: ElizaOS is undergoing a strategic shift to prioritize the 'Babylon' agent social media platform within TEE environments while managing significant community tension regarding token utility and migration transparency. Technical efforts are focused on stabilizing developer onboarding and expanding agent code execution capabilities.

GitHub Activity:

  • Fix elizaos create command and pathing (merged)
  • Broker Authentication for Twitter (merged)
  • Code Execution Plugin (closed)

Community Highlights:

  • #core-devs: Strategic pivot toward rapid deployment of Babylon as an agent social media platform in TEE to compete with Moltbook and leverage the Claude 5 hype cycle....
  • #coders: Discussion of ElizaCloud server-side bugs and authentication issues with OpenClaw agents. Documentation for A2A protocols was disseminated....
  • #discussion: Intense community debate regarding the 40% team token allocation, lack of ElizaCloud token utility, and project transparency....

item.title: elizaOS Intelligence: 2026-01-31
item.description:
Summary: The ElizaOS ecosystem is balancing critical technical stabilization, including fixes for project generation and v2.0 development, against significant community concerns regarding token migration eligibility and financial runway. Strategic integration with the Moltbot/openclaw network is being explored to expand the agent ecosystem into mobile and voice interfaces.

GitHub Activity:

  • V2.0.0 (open)
  • Can not generate project (closed)
  • Integrate N8N Workflow Engine (open)

Community Highlights:

  • #partners: Discussions focused on the 8-month financial runway and the trade-offs between decentralized sovereignty and centralized AI infrastructure security....
  • #coders: Technical comparison between ElizaOS (multi-agent) and Clawdbot (assistant). Discussion on Moltbook integration and mobile/voice interface gaps in Eliza....
  • #discussion: Dominated by migration troubleshooting and high-alert security warnings following a user hack. Updates on Jeju staking mechanics using ETH placeholders....

item.title: elizaOS Intelligence: 2026-01-30
item.description:
Summary: The project is navigating a phase of architectural expansion through n8n workflow integration and AI routing frameworks, while simultaneously managing community tension regarding token distribution and ETH-chain liquidity issues.

GitHub Activity:

  • V2.0.0 (open)
  • [Plugin] Integrate N8N Workflow Engine (open)
  • Can not generate project (closed)

Community Highlights:

  • #coders: Technical focus on AI routing systems (ElizaRouter) and debugging callback sequence errors in version 1.7.2....
  • #discussion: High-intensity debate regarding tokenomics, specifically the 40% team allocation and the lack of liquidity on the ETH chain migration....
  • #core-devs: Exploration of Composio for in-chat authentication and social media integration flows....

item.title: elizaOS Intelligence: 2026-01-29
item.description:
Summary: ElizaOS has pivoted strongly toward a multi-product strategy and workflow automation platform, integrating n8n as a core engine for AI agent tasks. Technical efforts are concurrently focused on resolving persistent OpenRouter embedding issues and stabilizing the v2.0.0 scoped CLI release.

GitHub Activity:

  • fix: update import statement in elizaos.js to use package alias (merged)
  • [Plugin] Integrate N8N Workflow Engine (open)
  • V2.0.0: dynamic execution engine (open)

Community Highlights:

  • #-partners: Serious legal and structural concerns were raised regarding the 'ClawdBot' FOMO dapp, characterizing it as a potential scam/illegal gambling operation. Discussions shifted to improving the @elizaos Tw...
  • #-coders: Troubleshooting centered on OpenRouter embedding failures, MIME_TYPE_MISMATCH errors in SSE streaming (with socket.io recommended as a fix), and the use of 'Composer 1' over Cursor's AUTO mode for dep...
  • core-devs: Development roadmap focused on developer onboarding (Time To Value), n8n workflow integration, and the implementation of MCP for repo activity data....
